title: rubus macrostachys var. militaris
intro: Rubus macrostachys var. militaris, also known as the thorny raspberry, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is native to the Andes mountains in South America, particularly in Peru and Bolivia, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Rubus macrostachys var. militaris include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, and it is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption.
